name: Roboflow
what_it_does: Computer vision platform API that enables agents to upload and annotate image datasets, train and deploy object detection and classification models, and run inference on images — covering the full CV model lifecycle from raw data to deployed endpoint.
use_cases: Uploading and annotating image datasets for custom object detection model training without manual labeling infrastructure
Running inference on images or video streams using a deployed Roboflow model endpoint via REST API
Generating augmented dataset versions with preprocessing and augmentation pipelines defined via API
Downloading datasets in YOLO, COCO, or Pascal VOC format for use in local training pipelines
Monitoring model performance and version-controlling CV models across training iterations
not_for: NLP or LLM workloads — Roboflow is exclusively for computer vision (image and video)
Real-time video processing at scale requiring sub-10ms latency — hosted inference adds network overhead
Teams needing fully on-premise CV infrastructure with no cloud dependency
best_when: You are building an agent that needs to process images, detect objects, or classify visual content and want a managed platform that handles dataset management, model training, and inference hosting.
avoid_when: Your use case requires custom model architectures not supported by Roboflow's training pipeline, or you need sub-10ms inference latency that requires on-device or edge deployment.

auth.notes: API key per workspace, passed as query parameter or Authorization header. Project-level and workspace-level keys available. Private model inference requires the API key; public models can be called without auth.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: 10,000 source images, 3 private projects, 1,000 monthly inference API calls
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter: $249/month; Grow: $749/month; Enterprise: custom. Inference hosted endpoint pricing is separate.
pricing.notes: Inference on Roboflow-hosted endpoints costs per call on paid tiers. Self-hosted inference via roboflow-inference package is free and open-source.

performance.rate_limits: Inference API: rate limited per plan. Dataset upload and management API: not strictly rate limited but throttled on free tier.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns JSON error objects with message fields. Inference API errors include model-specific context. Some dataset management endpoints return opaque 500 errors for edge cases.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Image upload is not idempotent — uploading the same image twice creates duplicate entries unless deduplication is configured at the dataset level.
known_agent_gotchas: Image upload requires multipart form data — agents must handle binary file encoding, not just JSON payloads
Model versions in Roboflow are immutable — updating a model creates a new version number; agents must track which version to call
Inference API returns bounding boxes in pixel coordinates relative to the uploaded image resolution — agents must handle coordinate normalization
Dataset version generation (with augmentations) is async and can take minutes — agents must poll for completion before downloading
Free tier inference API has strict concurrency limits — parallel image processing will hit rate limits quickly

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key passed as query param in some endpoints is a security antipattern (key may appear in server logs). Workspace and project-level key separation provides moderate granularity. SOC 2 compliant.
